reasoning: The United States has consistently tightened export controls on AI technologies (advanced chips, and recently model weights) over the past several years, with significant updates in late 2022, 2023, and late 2024/Jan 2025. The Biden administration left ongoing rulemakings, such as proposed "Know Your Customer" rules for U.S. cloud service providers (IaaS) to restrict foreign adversaries' access to cloud AI computing, which are ripe for finalization in 2025. While the new Trump administration generally emphasizes deregulation, it is highly hawkish on technological competition with China and is very likely to proceed with finalizing these cloud access restrictions or making further adjustments to the expanding AI model/chip rules. Given the breadth of the resolution criteria (covering chips, cloud access, OR model weights) and the momentum of the Commerce Department on these exact issues, it is highly likely (75%) that new or expanded controls in at least one of these sectors will take effect between May and December 2025.
